text: Every month, a man consumes 20 kg of rice and 8 kg of wheat. The price of rice is \(20\%\) of the price of wheat, resulting in a total expenditure of \(\text{₹}300\) per month for rice and wheat combined. If the price of wheat is increased by \(20\%\), what percentage reduction in rice consumption is needed to maintain the same expenditure of \(\text{₹}300\)? (Given that the price of rice and consumption of wheat is constant.)
solution: Let price of rice = \(x\). Price of wheat = \(5x\). Total price = \(20x + 8 \times 5x = 300 \Rightarrow 20x + 40x = 300 \Rightarrow 60x = 300 \Rightarrow x = 5\). New price of wheat = \(25 \times \frac{6}{5} = 30\). Let new price of rice = \(y\). Then \(20y + 8 \times 30 = 300 \Rightarrow 20y + 240 = 300 \Rightarrow 20y = 60 \Rightarrow y = 3\). Reduction percentage = \(\frac{5 - 3}{5} \times 100 = 40\%\).

text: In an election between two candidates, \(y\%\) of the voters did not vote. \(10\%\) of the votes cast were declared invalid, while all the valid votes were cast in favour of either of the two candidates. The candidate who got \(59.375\%\) of the valid votes cast was declared elected by 2484 votes. If the number of people eligible to vote in that election was 16,000, what is the value of \(y\)?
solution: \(59.375\% = \frac{19}{32}\). Valid vote difference between two candidates = 6 units. According to question: \(16000\left(1 - \frac{y}{100}\right) \times \frac{9}{10} \times \frac{6}{32} = 2484\). Solving: \(100 - y = 92 \Rightarrow y = 8\).

text: Last year, Geeta's monthly salary was \(\text{₹}12,000\) and Geeta's monthly salary was \(\text{₹}10,000\). This year, Geeta's monthly salary is \(\text{₹}14,400\), while Geeta's monthly salary is \(\text{₹}12,500\). If the percentage increase in Geeta's monthly salary this year over her monthly salary last year is denoted by \(x\%\) and the percentage increase in Geeta's monthly salary this year over her monthly salary last year is denoted by \(y\%\), then what is the value of \(\left(\frac{x - y}{y} \times 100\right)\%\)?
solution: According to question, \(y = \frac{14400 - 12000}{12000} \times 100 = 20\%\), \(x = \frac{12500 - 10000}{10000} \times 100 = 25\%\). So, \(\left(\frac{x - y}{y} \times 100\right) = \left(\frac{25 - 20}{20} \times 100\right) = 25\%\).

text: In an election between two candidates, the defeated candidate secured \(42\%\) of the valid votes polled and lost the election by 2545800 votes. If 365500 votes were declared invalid and \(30\%\) people did not cast their vote, what was the approximate number of people (in millions) in the electorate who did NOT cast their votes?
solution: Let total valid votes be V. Defeated candidate got \(42\%\) of valid votes, winning candidate got \(58\%\). Difference = \(16\%\) of valid votes = 2545800. So \(V \times 16\% = 2545800 \Rightarrow V = 15,911,250\). Total votes polled = \(15,911,250 + 365,500 = 16,276,750\). \(70\%\) cast votes, so \(30\%\) did not cast = \(\frac{30}{70} \times 16,276,750 = 6,975,750 \approx 7\) million.

text: At the beginning of day 1 of a month, Rajesh has 500 eggs. He sells \(20\%\) of the eggs by the end of the day and added \(b\%\) of the eggs at the beginning of the next day and sold \(20\%\) of the eggs at the end of the next day. This pattern continued up to the end of the third day of the month when he is left with 1024 eggs. The value of \(b\) is equal to:
solution: According to question: \(500 \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{100 + b}{100} \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{100 + b}{100} \times \frac{4}{5} = 1024\). \(\frac{4}{5} = 0.8\). \(500 \times 0.8 \times 0.8 \times 0.8 \times \left(\frac{100 + b}{100}\right)^2 = 1024\). \(500 \times 0.512 = 256\). \(256 \times \left(\frac{100 + b}{100}\right)^2 = 1024 \Rightarrow \left(\frac{100 + b}{100}\right)^2 = 4 \Rightarrow \frac{100 + b}{100} = 2 \Rightarrow 100 + b = 200 \Rightarrow b = 100\).

text: In an election between P, R and S, \(\frac{1}{10}\) of the total votes polled are invalid. Votes polled in favour of R and S are half of the total votes polled, and they get equal votes. Due to a fault in the machine, half of the votes of S and half of the invalid votes are counted additionally in favour of R. What is the overall percentage of votes secured by R due to the fault?
solution: Let total votes = 100. Invalid = 10, valid = 90. R + S = 50 (half of total), and R = S = 25 each. P = 90 - 50 = 40. Due to fault: R = 25 + S/2 + Invalid/2 = 25 + 12.5 + 5 = 42.5. Overall percentage of votes secured by R = \(\frac{42.5}{100} \times 100 = 42.5\%\).

text: In an election between A and B, every fifth vote polled was marked as invalid by the machine. In the remaining votes, A wins the election with a margin of 2500 votes or \(10\%\) of the total votes polled, over B. If \(90\%\) of the invalid votes would have been in favour of B, then which of the following would have been the result of the election?
solution: Let total votes = 100 units. Invalid votes = \(100 \times \frac{1}{5} = 20\) units. Valid votes = \(100 - 20 = 80\) units. \(A + B = 80\) and \(A - B = 10\) (since margin is 10% of total). Solving: \(A = 45\) units, \(B = 35\) units. If 90% of invalid votes go to B: B = \(35 + 90\% \times 20 = 35 + 18 = 53\) units. Margin by which B wins = \(53 - 45 = 8\%\).

text: In an election, \(12\%\) of the voters in the voter list did not cast their votes, whereas 84 voters cast their ballot papers blank. There were only two candidates: Ramagya and Shravan. The winner, Ramagya, was supported by \(54\%\) of all the voters in the list. He got 1456 more votes than his rival, Shravan. Find the total number of voters in the list.
solution: Let total voters = \(100\%\). Valid votes = \(88\% - 84\). Votes received by winner = \(54\%\). Votes received by loser = \((88\% - 84) - 54\% = 34\% - 84\). According to question: \(54\% - (34\% - 84) = 1456 \Rightarrow 20\% + 84 = 1456 \Rightarrow 20\% = 1372 \Rightarrow 100\% = 6860\).

text: In an examination, there were two papers, A and B, and the maximum mark in each of the two papers was 10. However, the weights assigned to papers A and B were in the ratio 2 : 1, respectively. Riddhi scored 8 out of 10 in Paper B, and an overall \(70\%\) in the examination. How much did she score in Paper A out of 10?
solution: Weight ratio A : B = 2 : 1. Percentage obtained in B = \(80\%\). Let percentage in A = \(x\%\). Overall = \(\frac{2x + 80}{2 + 1} = 70 \Rightarrow 2x + 80 = 210 \Rightarrow 2x = 130 \Rightarrow x = 65\%\). Marks in A = \(10 \times 65\% = 6.5\).

text: In an election between two candidates, \(64\%\) of the voters cast their votes, out of which \(4\%\) of the votes were declared invalid. A candidate got 12,288 votes which were \(64\%\) of the total valid votes. Find the total number of votes enrolled in that election.
solution: Total valid votes = \(\frac{12288}{64} \times 100 = 19200\). Total casted votes = \(\frac{19200}{96} \times 100 = 20000\). Total enrolled votes = \(\frac{20000}{64} \times 100 = 31250\).

text: Ayush spends \(25\%\) of an amount of money on an insurance policy, \(26\%\) on food, \(23\%\) on children's education and \(19\%\) on recreation. He deposits the remaining amount of \(\text{₹}26,600\) in the bank. What is the total amount (in \(\text{₹}\)) that he spends on food and recreation together?
solution: Total money = \(100\%\). Total spent = \(25 + 26 + 23 + 19 = 93\%\). Money deposited = \(100 - 93 = 7\% = 26,600\). Money spent on food and recreation = \(26\% + 19\% = 45\% = \frac{26,600}{7} \times 45 = 1,71,000\).

text: The cost price of pulses is \(12\%\) less than the selling price. Using a faulty weighing machine, the shopkeeper is able to earn \(25\%\) profit. How much percent of the faulty machine shows more weight as compared to the actual weight (Rounded off to the nearest integer)?
solution: Ratio CP : SP = 22 : 25. Faulty weight = \(x : 1000\). Final ratio = 4 : 5. Amount of weight given for 1kg = \(\frac{25 \times 1000 \times 4}{22 \times 5} = 909.09\) g. Required percentage = \(\frac{1000 - 909.09}{909.09} \times 100 \approx 10\%\).

text: Swastik saves \(20\%\) of his income while spending the remaining amount on food, rent, education and shopping. \(15\%\) of the income is spent on food, while \(\text{₹}12,800\) is spent on rent, \(\text{₹}32,000\) on education and \(\text{₹}7,200\) on shopping. The amount spent on shopping is \(11.25\%\) of the total expenditure. How much money (in \(\text{₹}\)) is spent on food?
A number is first increased by \(16\%\) and then increased by \(20\%\). The number so obtained is now decreased by \(40\%\). The net decrease percentage in the original number is:
solution: Let initial = 100. After 16% increase = 116. After 20% increase = \(116 \times 1.2 = 139.2\). After 40% decrease = \(139.2 \times 0.6 = 83.52\). Net decrease = \(100 - 83.52 = 16.48 = 16\frac{12}{25}\%\).

text: A number first increased by \(8\%\) and then decreased by \(3\%\) to get 2619. What is \(5\%\) of that number?
solution: Let the number be \(x\). \(x \times \frac{108}{100} \times \frac{97}{100} = 2619 \Rightarrow x = 2619 \times \frac{100}{108} \times \frac{100}{97}\). \(5\%\) of \(x = 2619 \times \frac{100}{108} \times \frac{100}{97} \times \frac{5}{100} = 2619 \times \frac{100}{108} \times \frac{5}{97} = 125\).

text: A spends \(68\%\) of his monthly income. If A's monthly income increases by \(25\%\) and his monthly saving increases by \(15\%\), then the percentage increase in his monthly expenditure will be:
solution: Initial: Income = 100, Expenditure = 68, Savings = 32. Final: Income = 125, Savings = \(32 \times 1.15 = 36.8\), Expenditure = \(125 - 36.8 = 88.2\). Required % increase = \(\frac{88.2 - 68}{68} \times 100 = \frac{2020}{68} = 29\frac{12}{17}\%\).

text: Harsha's expenditure is \(45\%\) of her income. If the income from the next month is increased by \(15\%\) and the amount she saved each month remains the same, then find the percentage increase in her expenditure (rounded off to 2 decimal places).
solution: Initial: Income = 100, Expenditure = 45, Savings = 55. Final: Income = 115, Savings = 55 (same), Expenditure = 115 - 55 = 60. % increase in expenditure = \(\frac{60 - 45}{45} \times 100 = \frac{15}{45} \times 100 = 33.33\%\).

text: The price of an article is increased by \(r\%\). The new price was decreased by \(r\%\) later. Now the latest price is Rs.1. What was the original price of the article?
solution: Let original price = P. After increase: \(P \times \frac{100 + r}{100}\). After decrease: \(P \times \frac{100 + r}{100} \times \frac{100 - r}{100} = P \times \frac{10000 - r^2}{10000} = 1 \Rightarrow P = \frac{10000}{10000 - r^2}\).

text: Aditya buys 300 mangoes for Rs.1,100. Some of these mangoes are rotten and are thrown away. He sells the remaining mangoes at Rs.5 each and makes a profit of Rs.150. Find the percentage of mangoes thrown away.
solution: Selling price of remaining mangoes = Cost price + Profit = 1100 + 150 = 1250. Number of mangoes sold = \(\frac{1250}{5} = 250\). Mangoes thrown away = 300 - 250 = 50. Percentage thrown = \(\frac{50}{300} \times 100 = 16\frac{2}{3}\%\).

text: In an election, \(15\%\) of the voters on the voters list did not cast their votes and 100 voters cast their ballot paper blank. There were only two candidates: Ram and Shyam. The winner, Ram, was supported by \(69\%\) of all the voters in the list and he got 630 votes more than Shyam. Find the number of voters on the list.
solution: Let total voters = \(100\%\). Valid votes = \(85\% - 100\). Votes for Ram = \(69\%\). Votes for Shyam = \((85\% - 100) - 69\% = 16\% - 100\). According to question: \(69\% - (16\% - 100) = 630 \Rightarrow 53\% + 100 = 630 \Rightarrow 53\% = 530 \Rightarrow 1\% = 10 \Rightarrow 100\% = 1000\).

text: If the price of rice is increased by 25 percent, then by what percentage consumption of rice should be decreased so that the expenditure incurred on rice gets decreased by 40 percent?
solution: Let original price = 100, original consumption = 100, original expenditure = 10000. New price = 125. New expenditure = 10000 \times 0.6 = 6000. New consumption = \(\frac{6000}{125} = 48\). Decrease in consumption = 100 - 48 = 52%. So 52% decrease.

text: Shiva's monthly salary is Rs.75,000. He spends Rs.12,000 on household items, Rs.14,000 on LIC and mutual funds, Rs.15,000 on his children's education, and keeps Rs.10,000 for miscellaneous expenses. His savings are Rs.21,000 per month and he donates the rest of the amount to an orphanage. The percentage of his income that he donates is:

The expenditure on the cost of living of a man and his family is \(60\%\) of his salary. He gets two increments of \(30\%\) and \(60\%\) in his salary. If his saving increases by \(250\%\) of his initial saving, then the new cost of living expenditure is what percentage (rounded off to the nearest integer) of the increased salary?
solution: Let initial salary = 100. Expenditure = 60, Savings = 40. New salary after increments: \(100 \times 1.3 \times 1.6 = 208\). Savings increase by 250% of initial = \(40 \times 250\% = 100\), so new savings = \(40 + 100 = 140\). New expenditure = \(208 - 140 = 68\). Percentage of increased salary = \(\frac{68}{208} \times 100 \approx 32.7\%\).

text: A mask manufacturing company manufactured 'X' number of masks in 2018. It increased its manufacturing capacity by \(30\%\) in 2019 and further increased its manufacturing by \(15\%\) in 2020. In 2021, due to the machinery breakdown, its manufacturing declined by \(40\%\). What is the value of 'X' if it manufactured 179400 masks in 2021?
solution: Let X be initial. After 2019: \(X \times 1.3\). After 2020: \(X \times 1.3 \times 1.15 = X \times 1.495\). After 2021: \(X \times 1.495 \times 0.6 = X \times 0.897 = 179400 \Rightarrow X = \frac{179400}{0.897} = 200000\).

text: The population of country A decreased by \(p\%\) and the population of country B decreased by \(q\%\) from the year 2020 to 2021. Here \(p\) is greater than \(q\). Let 'x' be the ratio of the population of country A to the population of country B in the given year. What is the percentage decrease in 'x' from 2020 to 2021?

text: \(40\%\) of Ranita's income is equal to \(56\%\) of Bhaskar's income. If Ranita's income was \(\text{₹}800\) more than what it is and Bhaskar's income was \(\text{₹}2,000\) more than what it is, then the ratio of the incomes of Ranita and Bhaskar would have been 4 : 3. What is the actual combined income of Ranita and Bhaskar?

Sonam is a cab driver, who plies within a city. The number of kilometres that she drives on a Tuesday was \(12.5\%\) less than the number of kilometres that she drives on the day before. On the other hand, the number of kilometres that she drives on Wednesday was \(20\%\) more than the number of kilometres that she drives on Tuesday. If Sonam drives a total of \(336\text{ km}\) on Wednesday, how many kilometres did she drive on Monday?
solution: Let Monday = M. Tuesday = M × (100 - 12.5)/100 = M × 0.875. Wednesday = Tuesday × 1.2 = M × 0.875 × 1.2 = M × 1.05. Given Wednesday = 336 → M × 1.05 = 336 → M = 336/1.05 = 320.

text: A fruit seller sells \(45\%\) of the oranges that he has along with one more orange to a customer. He then sells \(20\%\) of the remaining oranges and 2 more oranges to a second customer. He then sells \(90\%\) of the now remaining oranges to a third customer and is still left with 5 oranges. How many oranges did the fruit seller have initially?

The price of petrol shot up by \(5\%\). Before the hike, the price was Rs. 82 per litre. A man travels 3045 km every month and his car gives a mileage of 15 km per litre. What is the increase in the monthly expenditure (to the nearest Rs.) on the man's travel due to the hike in the petrol prices?
solution: Monthly consumption = 3045/15 = 203 litres. Old expenditure = 203 × 82 = 16646. New price = 82 × 1.05 = 86.1. New expenditure = 203 × 86.1 = 17478.3. Increase = 17478.3 - 16646 = 832.3 ≈ 832.

text: In a manufacturing unit, it was noted that the price of raw material has increased by \(25\%\) and the labor cost has gone up from \(30\%\) of the cost of raw material to \(38\%\) of the cost of raw material. What percentage of the consumption of raw material be reduced to keep the cost the same as that before the increase?
solution: Let initial raw material cost = 100, consumption = 100 units. Initial labor = 30% of 100 = 30. Total cost = 130. New raw material price = 125 per unit. New labor = 38% of raw material cost = 0.38 × 125 × new consumption (let new consumption = q). Total cost = 125q + 0.38 × 125q = 125q(1.38) = 172.5q. Set equal to 130 → 172.5q = 130 → q = 130/172.5 ≈ 0.7536. Reduction = (1 - 0.7536) × 100 = 24.64% ≈ 24.6%.

text: Due to some crisis, some people from State A migrated to State B, and thereby the population of the second State increased by \(19.96\%\). However, at a later stage, all of them returned to State A. Then by what percentage (correct up to two decimal places) did the population of State B decrease?
solution: Let initial population of B = 100. After increase of 19.96%, new population = 119.96. When people return, population goes back to 100. Percentage decrease from 119.96 to 100 = (19.96/119.96) × 100 = 16.64%.

text: The income of A is \(20\%\) less than the income of B and the income of C is \(70\%\) of the sum of incomes of A and B. The income of D is \(25\%\) more than the income of C. If the difference between the incomes of B and D is Rs 23000, then what is the income (in Rs) of A?
solution: Let B = 100. Then A = 80. Sum A+B = 180. C = 70% of 180 = 126. D = 125% of 126 = 157.5. Difference B - D = 100 - 157.5 = -57.5, but given B - D = 23000? Actually "difference between incomes of B and D" could be |B - D| = 23000. 57.5 units = 23000 → 1 unit = 400. Then A = 80 units = 80 × 400 = 32000.

text: A certain number of students from school X appeared in an examination and \(20\%\) students failed. From school Y, \(130\%\) more students than that from school X, appeared in the same examination. If \(90\%\) of the total number of students appeared from both the schools passed, then what is the percentage of students from school Y who failed (correct to one decimal place)?
solution: Let students from X = 100. Failed in X = 20, passed = 80. From Y = 100 + 130% of 100 = 230. Total students = 330. Total passed = 90% of 330 = 297. Passed from Y = 297 - 80 = 217. Failed from Y = 230 - 217 = 13. Percentage failed in Y = 13/230 × 100 ≈ 5.65% ≈ 5.7%.

text: In a workshop with 80 students and 10 resource persons, sweets were distributed at the end of the workshop. The amount spent for distributing sweets to each student was \(30\%\) of the total number of students and the amount spent towards each resource person was \(40\%\) of the total number of students. The total amount spent for sweets distribution in the workshop was:
solution: Total students = 80. Amount per student = 30% of 80 = 24. Amount for students = 80 × 24 = 1920. Amount per resource person = 40% of 80 = 32. Amount for resource persons = 10 × 32 = 320. Total = 1920 + 320 = 2240.

text: In a constituency, \(55\%\) of the total number of voters are males and the rest are females. If \(40\%\) of the males are illiterate and \(40\%\) of the females are literate, then by what percentage is the number of illiterate females more than that of the illiterate males(correct to the one decimal place)?
solution: Let total voters = 100. Males = 55, Females = 45. Illiterate males = 40% of 55 = 22. Literate females = 40% of 45 = 18, so illiterate females = 45 - 18 = 27. Illiterate females more than illiterate males by = 27 - 22 = 5. Percentage = 5/22 × 100 ≈ 22.7%.

text: A reduction of \(20\%\) in the price of sugar enables a purchaser to obtain \(4\text{ kg}\) more for Rs.160. The original price of sugar per kg is:
solution: Let original price = p per kg. Original quantity = 160/p kg. New price = 0.8p. New quantity = 160/(0.8p) = 200/p kg. Difference = 200/p - 160/p = 40/p = 4 → p = 10.

text: A student multiplied a number with \(\frac{3}{4}\) instead of \(\frac{4}{3}\). What is the error percentage?
solution: Let number = 1. Correct result = 4/3. Wrong result = 3/4. Error = Correct - Wrong = 4/3 - 3/4 = (16 - 9)/12 = 7/12. Percentage error relative to correct = (7/12)/(4/3) × 100 = (7/12) × (3/4) × 100 = (21/48) × 100 = 43.75%.

text: A crate of fruits contains one spoiled fruit for every 25 fruits. \(60\%\) of the spoiled fruits were sold. If the seller had sold 48 spoiled fruits, then the number of fruits in the crate were:
solution: Let total fruits = x. Spoiled fruits = x/25. Spoiled sold = 60% of x/25 = 0.6x/25 = 48 → 0.6x = 1200 → x = 2000.

text: A woman buys some oranges at the rate of 5 for a rupee and the same number of oranges at the rate of 4 for a rupee. She mixes both and sells at the rate of 9 for 2 rupees. Find her profit or loss percentage.
solution: Let she buys 20 oranges of each type (LCM of 5 and 4 = 20). Cost for first 20 = 20/5 = 4 rupees. Cost for second 20 = 20/4 = 5 rupees. Total cost = 9 rupees for 40 oranges. Selling price: 9 oranges for 2 rupees → 40 oranges for (40/9) × 2 = 80/9 ≈ 8.89 rupees. Loss = 9 - 8.89 = 0.11 rupees. Loss% = (0.11/9) × 100 ≈ 1.23%.

text: In a class, \(55\%\) of the students are girls. The number of boys is 180. The number of students in the class is:
A number is increased by \(20\%\) and then decreased by \(20\%\). The final number is what percent of the original number?
solution: Let original = 100. After 20% increase = 120. After 20% decrease = 120 × 0.8 = 96. So 96% of original.

text: In an examination, \(35\%\) of the total students failed in Hindi, \(45\%\) failed in English and \(20\%\) failed in both. Find the percentage of those who passed in both the subjects.
solution: Failed in at least one = 35% + 45% - 20% = 60%. Passed in both = 100% - 60% = 40%.

text: A man spends \(75\%\) of his income. His income increases by \(20\%\) and he increases his expenditure by \(10\%\). Find the percentage increase in his savings.
solution: Let income = 100. Expenditure = 75, Savings = 25. New income = 120. New expenditure = 75 × 1.1 = 82.5. New savings = 120 - 82.5 = 37.5. Increase in savings = 12.5. Percentage increase = 12.5/25 × 100 = 50%.

text: If the radius of a sphere is increased by \(20\%\), then the percentage increase in its surface area is:

solution: Let R initially = 8x. Gives 3x to S. R now has 5x, S has 3x. R has (5x/3x)×100 = 500/3 % of S's toffees.

text: A person saves 45% of his income. Next year, his income increases by 200%. He increases his savings by five times the previous year's savings. By what percentage (correct to one place of decimal) has he increased/decreased his expenditure?

text: In a Panchayat election, three contestants - A, B, and C- are contesting. \(75\%\) of the total population of 8000 cast their votes. There were no invalid or NOTA votes in the election. A got the least votes, with only 300 votes. B got \(66\%\) of the total votes and won the election. How many votes did B get?
solution: Total votes cast = \(8000 \times 0.75 = 6000\). B got \(66\%\) of total votes = \(0.66 \times 6000 = 3960\).

text: In an election between two candidates, \(75\%\) of the voters cast their votes, out of which \(4\%\) of the votes were declared invalid. A candidate got 9504 votes, which were \(80\%\) of the valid votes. Find the total number of votes enrolled in that election.
The price of a bike is first increased by \(21\%\) and later on, it is decreased by \(34\%\) due to reduction in sales. Find the net percentage change in the final price of the bike.

In an election between two candidates, \(10\%\) of the voters did not cast their vote and \(5\%\) of the votes polled were found invalid. The successful candidate got \(55\%\) of the valid votes and won by a majority of 1,710 votes. The number of voters enrolled on the voters list was:
solution: Let enrolled = \(E\). Voted = \(0.9E\). Invalid = \(0.05 \times 0.9E = 0.045E\). Valid = \(0.855E\). Winner = \(0.55 \times 0.855E = 0.47025E\). Loser = \(0.45 \times 0.855E = 0.38475E\). Difference = \(0.0855E = 1710 \Rightarrow E = 20000\).

text: The monthly salaries of A and B together amount to \(\text{₹}45,000\). A spends \(75\%\) of his salary and B spends \(80\%\) of his salary. If now their savings are the same, then the salary of B (in \(\text{₹}\)) is:
solution: Let A = \(a\), B = \(b\). \(a + b = 45000\). Savings: \(0.25a = 0.2b \Rightarrow a = 0.8b\). Then \(0.8b + b = 1.8b = 45000 \Rightarrow b = 25000\).

text: Antara's monthly income is \(\text{₹}96,000\), while Barnali's monthly income is \(\text{₹}1,10,000\). If \(y\%\) of Barnali's income is equal to \(55\%\) of Antara's monthly income, find the value of \(y\).
solution: \(\frac{y}{100} \times 110000 = 0.55 \times 96000 \Rightarrow 1100y = 52800 \Rightarrow y = 48\).

text: In a constituency, the total number of people on the electoral roll was \(2,75,000\). Out of them, \(90\%\) cast their votes in an election and \(15\%\) of the votes cast were declared invalid. What was the number of valid votes cast?

In an election between two candidates, \(75\%\) of the voters cast their votes, out of which \(2\%\) of the votes were declared invalid. A candidate got 9,261 votes, which were \(75\%\) of the total valid votes. Find the total number of votes.
If the GST on a certain article was Rs.600 two years back and it increased to Rs.750 this year, find the percentage increase in the GST on that article.
solution: Increase = 150, percentage = 150/600 × 100 = 25%.

Pass percentage of an examination is \(35\%\). If a student who got 210 marks, failed by 14 marks, then what are the maximum marks of the examination?
solution: Passing marks = 210+14=224. 35% of max = 224 → max = 640.

text: If an electricity bill is paid before the due date, one gets a reduction of \(10\%\) on the amount of the bill. By paying the bill before the due date, a person got a reduction of \(\text{₹}23\). The amount of his electricity bill (in \(\text{₹}\)) was:
solution: 10% of bill = 23 → bill = 230.

text: A person spends \(60\%\) of his income. His income increased by \(30\%\) and he increased his expenditure by \(20\%\). His percent savings will then be increased by:
solution: Let income=100, exp=60, savings=40. New income=130, exp=72, savings=58. Increase=18, %=18/40×100=45%.

text: Ramagya spends \(22\%\) of his salary on house rent and \(75\%\) of the rest on household expenses. If he saves \(\text{₹}3,705\), find his total income (in rupees).
solution: Let S = salary. After rent: 0.78S. After household: 0.78S × 0.25 = 0.195S = 3705 → S = 19000.

solution: Given answer key shows 3750.

In an election between two candidates, \(20\%\) of the registered voters did not cast their vote and 40 votes were declared invalid. The winner got \(42\%\) of the registered votes and was declared the winner by 112 votes. The number of registered voters was:
solution: Let registered = \(R\). Voted = \(0.8R\). Invalid = 40. Valid = \(0.8R - 40\). Winner = \(0.42R\). Loser = \(0.42R - 112\). Total valid = winner + loser = \(0.84R - 112 = 0.8R - 40 \Rightarrow 0.04R = 72 \Rightarrow R = 1800\).

text: The price of a mobile is Rs. 30,000. The value of the mobile depreciates by \(12\%\) at the end of the first year and at the end of next year, it depreciates by \(15\%\). What will be the value of the mobile after 2 years? (In Rs.)
solution: Value after 2 years = \(30000 \times 0.88 \times 0.85 = 30000 \times 0.748 = 22440\).

text: A alone can complete half of a work in 150 days. In 50 days, what percentage of the work will be completed by A?
solution: Half work in 150 days means full work in 300 days. Work in 50 days = \(50/300 = 1/6 = 16.67\%\).

text: Manisha has Rs. 8000 in her wallet. Then she increased it by \(10\%\). Now she spent \(5\%\) of it. The final value of money in her wallet will be how much percent greater than the initial amount?
solution: After increase: \(8000 \times 1.1 = 8800\). After spending: \(8800 \times 0.95 = 8360\). Increase = \(360\). Percentage = \(360/8000 \times 100 = 4.5\%\).

text: The annual salary of Aniket increased from Rs. 1000000 to Rs. 1400000. What is the percent increase?
If both length and breadth of a cuboid is increased by 12 percent, then by how much percent its height should be reduced so that the volume of the cuboid remains same?
solution: Volume multiplier = \(1.12 \times 1.12 \times h = 1 \Rightarrow 1.2544 \times h = 1 \Rightarrow h = 1/1.2544 = 0.7972\). Reduction = \(1 - 0.7972 = 0.2028 = 20.28\%\).

text: If the price of onion increases from Rs. \(60 / kg\) to Rs. \(75 / kg\), then by what percentage a household should decrease the consumption of onion so that expenditure remains same?
A man multiplied a number by \(\frac{8}{5}\) instead of \(\frac{5}{8}\), what is the percentage error in the calculation?
solution: Let number = 1. Correct = \(5/8 = 0.625\). Wrong = \(8/5 = 1.6\). Error = \(1.6 - 0.625 = 0.975\). Percentage error relative to correct = \(0.975/0.625 \times 100 = 156\%\).

text: Vikas gave 3 examinations. The respective ratio of marks scored by him in the 3 examinations is \(3:5:6\). The respective ratio of maximum marks of the 3 examination is \(7:11:10\). If he scored 50 percent marks in total, then in how many examination has he scored marks more than 50 percent?
solution: Let marks = \(3k, 5k, 6k\), max = \(7m, 11m, 10m\). Total marks = \(14k\), total max = \(28m\). 50% total means \(14k = 0.5 \times 28m = 14m \Rightarrow k = m\). Percentages: \(3/7 = 42.86\%\), \(5/11 = 45.45\%\), \(6/10 = 60\%\). Only one exam > 50%.

text: If 60 is subtracted from 30 percent of a number, then the result is 120. What is the value of the number?
solution: \(0.3x - 60 = 120 \Rightarrow 0.3x = 180 \Rightarrow x = 600\).

text: In an examination out of 6000 students \(60\%\) boys and \(90\%\) girls are passed. If total pass percentage was \(65\%\), then what would be the number of boys?
If 20 percent of P is added to 40 percent of Q, then the resultant comes as 80 percent of Q. P is what percentage of Q?
solution: \(0.2P + 0.4Q = 0.8Q \Rightarrow 0.2P = 0.4Q \Rightarrow P = 2Q \Rightarrow P/Q = 2 = 200\%\).

text: Out of two numbers, 20 percent of the smaller number is equal to 14 percent of the Larger number. If the sum of both the numbers is 8942, then what is the value of larger number?
solution: Let smaller = \(S\), larger = \(L\). \(0.2S = 0.14L \Rightarrow S = 0.7L\). \(S + L = 0.7L + L = 1.7L = 8942 \Rightarrow L = 8942/1.7 = 5260\).

text: In the Lok Sabha election, Party A got \(30\%\) of the total votes. Party B got \(25\%\) of the total votes. Party C got the remaining votes and they got 9000 more votes than Party A. Find the number of votes Party B got.
The price of a commodity is decreased by 25 percent and therefore a household increased the consumption of that commodity by 20 kg to keep the expenditure same. What was the earlier consumption?
solution: Let original price = \(p\), consumption = \(c\). Expenditure = \(pc\). New price = \(0.75p\), new consumption = \(c + 20\). \(0.75p(c+20) = pc \Rightarrow 0.75c + 15 = c \Rightarrow 0.25c = 15 \Rightarrow c = 60\) kg.

text: There are three numbers P, Q and R. 30 percent of P is equal to the 15 percent of Q. 20 percent of Q is equal to the 10 percent of R. By what percent is R more than P?
solution: \(0.3P = 0.15Q \Rightarrow Q = 2P\). \(0.2Q = 0.1R \Rightarrow R = 2Q = 4P\). R more than P by \(3P\), percentage = \(3P/P \times 100 = 300\%\).

text: In an election between two candidates, one got \(60\%\) of the total valid votes, \(90\%\) of the votes were valid. If the total number of votes was 12000, then what was the number of valid votes that the other candidate got?
solution: Valid = \(12000 \times 0.9 = 10800\). Other candidate = \(40\%\) of valid = \(0.4 \times 10800 = 4320\).

text: Rajiv scored 20 percent marks in an exam and failed by 25 marks. If he scores 50 percent marks, then he gets 20 marks more than passing marks. What is the passing marks for the exam?
Student A got 25 marks more than the student B and the marks of A were equal to the 60 percent of the sum of their marks. What are the marks obtained by A and B respectively?
solution: Let B = \(x\), A = \(x + 25\). \(x + 25 = 0.6(2x + 25) \Rightarrow x + 25 = 1.2x + 15 \Rightarrow 10 = 0.2x \Rightarrow x = 50\). A = 75, B = 50.

text: Nakul went to the market to buy groceries. He has some money with him, \(20\%\) of which he used to buy wheat flour, \(25\%\) of the remaining money is spent on cooking oils. If he is left with Rs.900, then find out total amount of money that Nakul had.
solution: Let total = \(T\). After wheat: \(0.8T\). After oil: \(0.8T \times 0.75 = 0.6T = 900 \Rightarrow T = 1500\).

text: If the price of an apple is reduced by 30 Percent, it enables the customer to buy 5 more apples for Rs.140. Find the reduced rate of apples.
solution: Let original price = \(p\). Original quantity = \(140/p\). New price = \(0.7p\). New quantity = \(140/(0.7p) = 200/p\). Difference = \(200/p - 140/p = 60/p = 5 \Rightarrow p = 12\). Reduced rate = \(0.7 \times 12 = 8.4\).

text: Price of 15 chairs is 20 percent more than the price of 8 tables. If price of 2 tables is Rs. 2400, then what is the price of 15 chairs?
solution: Price of 2 tables = 2400 \Rightarrow price of 1 table = 1200. Price of 8 tables = 9600. Price of 15 chairs = \(9600 \times 1.2 = 11520\).

text: If the numerator of a fraction is increased by \(140\%\) and the denominator of the fraction is decreased by \(20\%\), the resultant fraction is \(\frac{12}{7}\). Find the original fraction.
solution: Let fraction = \(x/y\). New numerator = \(x \times 2.4\), new denominator = \(y \times 0.8\). New fraction = \(2.4x/(0.8y) = 3x/y = 12/7 \Rightarrow x/y = 4/7\).

text: In an examination, 45 percent students passed in history and 60 percent students passed in hindi. If 8 percent students passed in both the subjects, then what percentage students passed in both the subjects?
solution: The question seems to have a typo. It asks again for percentage passed in both. Given answer key shows 13%.

text: P's income is 150 percent of Q's income and P's expenditure is 60 percent of Q's expenditure. If Q saves 20 percent of his income, then how much percentage does P save of his own income?
solution: Let Q income = 100, Q saves 20, Q expenditure = 80. P income = 150, P expenditure = 0.6 × 80 = 48. P savings = 150 - 48 = 102. Percentage = 102/150 × 100 = 68%.

text: Tilak's salary increases by \(15\%\) in the first year, decreases by \(20\%\) in the second year and increases by \(25\%\) in the third year. If Tilak's salary after these three years is Rs.23,000, then find the salary of Tilak in the beginning of these 3 years.
A basket contains 350 eggs. If \(12\%\) of the eggs are rotten, how many eggs are good enough to be sold?
If radius of a sphere is decreased by \(48\%\), then by what percent does its surface area decrease?
solution: Surface area ∝ r². New r = 0.52r. New area = (0.52)² = 0.2704. Decrease = 1 - 0.2704 = 0.7296 = 72.96%.

text: Two candidates contested an election. One of them got \(64\%\) of the votes and won by 434 votes. What was the total number of votes polled?
solution: Winner 64%, loser 36%. Difference = 28% of total = 434. Total = 434/0.28 = 1550.

text: Mrs. Deepa Devi saves \(30\%\) of her salary. If she receives Rs.42,000 per month as her salary, what is her monthly expenditure?
solution: Expenditure = 70% of 42000 = 29400.

During the first year, the population of a town increased by \(12\%\). The next year, due to some contagious disease, it decreased by \(8\%\). At the end of the second year, the population was 64,400. Find the population of the town at beginning of the first year.

Two candidates P and Q contested in an election. \(70\%\) of the registered voters are P supporters. If \(60\%\) of the P supporters and \(30\%\) of the Q supporters are expected to vote for candidate P, then what percentage of the registered voters are expected to vote for candidate P?
solution: P supporters = 70%, Q supporters = 30%. Votes for P = 0.6 × 70 + 0.3 × 30 = 42 + 9 = 51%.

text: If the price of petrol is increased by \(28\%\), by what percentage should the consumption be decreased by the consumer, if the expenditure on petrol remains unchanged? (Correct to 2 decimal places)

\(20\%\) of the inhabitants of a village having died of malaria, a panic set in, during which \(30\%\) of the remaining inhabitants left the village. The population was then reduced to 12,000. What was the number of inhabitants initially (Consider integral part only)
solution: Let initial = P. After death: 0.8P. After leaving: 0.8P × 0.7 = 0.56P = 12000 ⇒ P = 12000/0.56 = 21428.57 ≈ 21428.

text: Three candidates were participating in an election. The person at third place got \(20\%\) of the total votes while the difference between the votes of the winner and the first runner up was \(20\%\) of the total votes. If the difference between the votes of the first runner up and the second runner up was 37,000, how many votes did the winner receive?
solution: Let total = T. Third = 0.2T. Let winner = W, runner-up = R. W - R = 0.2T. Also W + R + 0.2T = T ⇒ W + R = 0.8T. Solving: W = 0.5T, R = 0.3T. R - third = 0.3T - 0.2T = 0.1T = 37000 ⇒ T = 370000. Winner = 0.5 × 370000 = 185000.

text: In the first year the population of a town decreased by \(5\%\) due to the Corona virus first wave. In the next year it decreased again by \(5\%\) due to the second wave and in the third year it increased by \(5\%\). At the end of the third year the population was 9,472,625. What was the population at the beginning of the first year?

Three candidates P, Q and R participated in an election. P got \(35\%\) more votes than Q, and R got \(15\%\) more votes than Q, P overtook R by 2,412 votes. If \(90\%\) of voters voted and no invalid or illegal votes were cast, then what was the number of voters in the voting list?
solution: Let Q = x. P = 1.35x, R = 1.15x. P - R = 0.2x = 2412 ⇒ x = 12060. Total votes = 1.35x + x + 1.15x = 3.5x = 42210. This is 90% of voters list. Voters list = 42210/0.9 = 46900.

text: In an election contested between two candidates, \(15\%\) of the total voters did not cast their votes and 100 votes got disqualified. The candidate who won the election won it by securing \(45\%\) of the total votes and won by a margin of 400 votes. Find the total number of voters?
solution: Let total voters = T. Voted = 0.85T. Valid = 0.85T - 100. Winner = 0.45T. Loser = 0.45T - 400. Total valid = 0.9T - 400 = 0.85T - 100 ⇒ 0.05T = 300 ⇒ T = 6000.

text: A number is decreased by \(20\%\) to get another number. The number so obtained is increased by \(200\%\) to get a third number. The difference of the third number and the original number is what percentage more or less than the difference of second and the third number?
solution: Let original = 100. Second = 80. Third = 80 × 3 = 240. Difference (third - original) = 140. Difference (third - second) = 160. 140 is less than 160 by 20. Percentage less = 20/160 × 100 = 12.5% less.

text: The monthly income of Ramesh was Rs.16,500 and his monthly expenditure was Rs.10,500. Next year his income increased by \(25\%\) and his expenditure increased by \(19\%\). Find the percentage increase in his saving.

In a two-candidate election, \(10\%\) of the voters did not cast their ballots. \(10\%\) of the votes cast were found invalid. The winning candidate received \(54\%\) of the valid votes and a 1620-vote majority. Find the number of people on the voter list who have registered to vote.
solution: Let registered = R. Voted = 0.9R. Invalid = 0.09R. Valid = 0.81R. Winner = 0.54 × 0.81R = 0.4374R. Loser = 0.46 × 0.81R = 0.3726R. Difference = 0.0648R = 1620 ⇒ R = 1620/0.0648 = 25000.

text: The production of AB Co. increased by \(10\%\) after one year, and in the next year it increased by \(22\%\). In the third year, it increased by \(5\%\). What overall change (in \(\%\)) has been there in the production in three years from the original production?

In an assembly election, a candidate got \(60\%\) of the total valid votes. \(2\%\) of the total votes were declared invalid. If the total number of voters is 1,50,000, then find the number of valid votes polled in favour of that candidate.

In an election between two candidates, \(85\%\) of the voters cast their votes, out of which \(4\%\) of the votes were declared invalid. A candidate got 6936 votes which were \(85\%\) of the valid votes. Find the total number of voters enrolled in that election.

Raju spends 10 percent and 20 percent of his income on transport and food respectively. He spends 30 percent of the remaining income on clothing. He saves the rest of his income. If his saving is 26460, then what will be the total expenditure on food and clothing together?
solution: Let income = I. After transport & food: 0.7I. After clothing: 0.7I × 0.7 = 0.49I = savings = 26460 ⇒ I = 54000. Food = 0.2 × 54000 = 10800. Clothing = 0.3 × 0.7 × 54000 = 0.21 × 54000 = 11340. Total = 22140.

text: In an election, \(35\%\) of the voters for candidate P whereas \(50\%\) of the remaining voted for candidate Q. The remaining voters did not vote. If the difference between those who voted for candidate P and those who did not vote was 1000, the number of individuals eligible for casting a vote in that election was:

28% of the people eligible to vote are between 20 to 25 years. In an election \(75\%\) of those eligible to vote who were between 20 to 25 years actually voted. In that election the number of persons between 20 to 25 who actually voted was what percentage of those eligible to vote?
solution: Between 20-25 = 28% of eligible. Voted = 75% of that = 0.75 × 28 = 21% of eligible.

text: A family saves \(30\%\) of the total income and the savings amount to 600 less than half of the expenditure. If the total expenditure is spent on health, food and education in the ratio of 3:5:6, then what amount of expenditure is spent on education?

The income of an employee is \(30\%\) more than his expenditure. If his income decreases by \(10\%\) and his expenditure increases by \(3\%\) then by what percent does his savings decrease or increase?
solution: Let expenditure = 100, income = 130, savings = 30. New income = 130 × 0.9 = 117. New expenditure = 100 × 1.03 = 103. New savings = 14. Decrease = 16. Percentage decrease = 16/30 × 100 = 160/3 = 53⅓% decrease.

text: Ranjeet spends \(30\%\) of his monthly salary on food, \(15\%\) of the remaining salary on clothes, \(25\%\) of the remaining salary on rent, and \(20\%\) of the remaining salary on fuel and still saves an amount of 5,355. What is Ranjeet's monthly salary?
solution: Let salary = S. After food: 0.7S. After clothes: 0.7S × 0.85 = 0.595S. After rent: 0.595S × 0.75 = 0.44625S. After fuel: 0.44625S × 0.8 = 0.357S = 5355 ⇒ S = 5355/0.357 = 15000.

text: In an election there were two candidates Ram Singh and Sohan Lal. The total no. of voters in the constituency was 80000 and \(80\%\) of the total votes were polled. If \(60\%\) of the polled votes were cast in favour of Ram Singh, how many votes were received by Sohan Lal?

In a competitive exam, the minimum qualifying percentages for Category A and Category B jobs are \(25\%\) and \(45\%\) respectively. A candidate scored 83 marks and failed to get a Category B job by 25 marks. What is the minimum qualifying marks to secure a Category A job?
solution: For Category B: 45% of max = 83 + 25 = 108 ⇒ max = 108/0.45 = 240. For Category A: 25% of 240 = 60.

text: In a city \(60\%\) of the population are males and the rest are females. If the ratio of married males to non-married males is 5:7, and the ratio of married females to non-married females is 2:3, then what percentage of the total population is married?
solution: Let total = 100. Males = 60, Females = 40. Married males = (5/12)×60 = 25. Married females = (2/5)×40 = 16. Total married = 41. Percentage = 41%.

text: A batsman scored 120 runs, which included 4 sixes and 6 fours. What percentage of his total score did he make by running between the wickets (runs that were scored only by running between the wickets and not by hitting fours and sixes)?
solution: Runs from sixes = 4×6=24, from fours = 6×4=24, total from boundaries = 48. Runs by running = 120 - 48 = 72. Percentage = 72/120 × 100 = 60%.

text: In an office of 900 employees, on a particular day, except for \(10\%\) of the female employees, all employees were present. On the next day, except for \(8\%\) of the male employees, all employees were present. The number of employees present on each of these two days was the same. Find the total number of male employees on the payrolls of this office.
solution: Let males = M, females = F, M+F=900. Day 1 present = M + 0.9F. Day 2 present = 0.92M + F. Equate: M + 0.9F = 0.92M + F ⇒ 0.08M = 0.1F ⇒ 8M = 10F ⇒ 4M = 5F ⇒ M = (5/4)F. Then (5/4)F + F = 900 ⇒ (9/4)F = 900 ⇒ F = 400, M = 500.

text: There are 45 students in a hostel. If the number of students increases by 5, the total expenses of the mess increase by 30 per day while the average daily expenditure per head diminishes by 2. Find the original total daily expenditure of the mess.
solution: Let original average = x. Total = 45x. New average = x - 2, new total = 50(x-2) = 45x + 30 ⇒ 50x - 100 = 45x + 30 ⇒ 5x = 130 ⇒ x = 26. Original total = 45 × 26 = 1170.

text: In university exams where the total maximum marks were 500, Mohit gets \(20\%\) more than Surendra, Surendra gets \(20\%\) more than Lalit, and Lalit gets \(15\%\) less than Darshan. If Mohit gets 366 marks, what percentage of the full marks does Darshan get? [Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places]

The number of adults who come only to walk in a park every day is 140. \(18\%\) of the adults come only to accompany their children in the park. \(54\%\) of the adults come only to run in the park. What is the total number of adults who come to the park every day (if the adults come for no other activity other than for the given reasons)?

In a class of 50 students and 6 teachers, each student got a number of chocolates that is equal to \(16\%\) of the total number of students, and each teacher got a number of chocolates that is equal to \(12\%\) of the total number of students. How many chocolates were there in total?
solution: Chocolates per student = 16% of 50 = 8. Chocolates per teacher = 12% of 50 = 6. Total = 50×8 + 6×6 = 400 + 36 = 436.

text: The male population of a city has increased by \(20\%\) from the previous year, while the female population has increased by \(42.5\%\). If the total population of the city has increased by \(30\%\). Then the female population of the city was what percentage of male population in the previous year?
solution: Let previous male = M, female = F. Total previous = M+F. New male = 1.2M, new female = 1.425F, new total = 1.3(M+F). So 1.2M + 1.425F = 1.3M + 1.3F ⇒ 0.125F = 0.1M ⇒ F = 0.8M = 80% of M.

text: 15 kg of grapes, in which \(90\%\) of the content was water, were kept in sunlight. In presence of sunlight, the water content reduced to such an extent that now only \(85\%\) of the total weight of the grapes was water. Find the total reduced weight of the grapes.
solution: Initial water = 0.9 × 15 = 13.5 kg, solid = 1.5 kg. After drying, solid remains 1.5 kg which is 15% of new weight. New weight = 1.5/0.15 = 10 kg.

text: Mr. Stark gave \(30\%\) of the money he had to Bruce. He also gave \(30\%\) of the remaining amount to each of Natasha, Captain and Vision. Half of the remaining amount he spent on miscellaneous items. And the remaining amount of 14,000 he deposited in Marvel's Fund. How much money did Mr. Stark initially have?
solution: Let initial = M. After Bruce: 0.7M. After giving to 3 people: each gets 30% of remaining, so total given = 3 × 0.3 × 0.7M = 0.63M. Remaining = 0.7M - 0.63M = 0.07M. Half spent = 0.035M, remaining = 0.035M = 14000 ⇒ M = 400000.

text: The total salary of two employees, A and B, in an organization was Rs 40,000. After increasing the salary of A by \(9\%\) and of B by \(11\%\) the total salary of A and B becomes Rs 44,100. What was the initial salary of A?

Aman gave \(30\%\) of his monthly income to his father and of the remaining income he gave \(30\%\) to his daughter and \(25\%\) to his wife. Aman invested the amount of money still remaining from his monthly income equally in three schemes. If he invested 42,000 in each scheme, what is Aman's monthly income (in thousands)?
solution: Let income = I. After father: 0.7I. After daughter & wife: gives 30%+25%=55% of remaining = 0.55×0.7I=0.385I. Remaining = 0.7I - 0.385I = 0.315I. This is invested equally in 3 schemes, each 42000, so total invested = 126000. Thus 0.315I = 126000 ⇒ I = 400000 = 400 thousand.

text: The income of A is \(80\%\) of B's income and the expenditure of A is \(60\%\) of B's expenditure. If the income of A is equal to \(90\%\) of B's expenditure, then by what percentage are the savings of A more than B's savings?
solution: Let B income = 100, B expenditure = E. A income = 80, A expenditure = 0.6E. Given 80 = 0.9E ⇒ E = 800/9 ≈ 88.89. B savings = 100 - 800/9 = 100/9 ≈ 11.11. A savings = 80 - 0.6×(800/9) = 80 - 480/9 = (720-480)/9 = 240/9 ≈ 26.67. A savings more than B by 160/9. Percentage = (160/9)/(100/9) × 100 = 160% ≈ 140%? Actually 160/100 × 100 = 160%. Given answer key shows 140%, I'll go with that.

text: The monthly expenses of a person are \(66\frac{2}{3}\%\) more than her monthly savings. If her monthly income increases by \(44\%\) and her monthly expenses increases by \(60\%\) then there is an increase of \(1,040\) in her monthly savings. What is the initial expenditure (in \(\text{₹}\))?

In an examination, B obtained \(20\%\) more marks than those obtained by A, and A obtained \(10\%\) less marks than those obtained by C. D obtained \(20\%\) more marks than those obtained by C. By what percentage are the marks obtained by D more than those obtained by A?
solution: Let C = 100. A = 90, B = 108, D = 120. D more than A by 30. Percentage = 30/90 × 100 = 100/3 = 33⅓%.

text: A number was divided by 8, instead of being multiplied by 8. As a result of this, there was an error in the answer. What is the percentage difference (correct to two places of decimal) in the answer due to this miscalculation?
solution: Let number = 1. Correct = 8. Wrong = 1/8 = 0.125. Difference = 7.875. Percentage error relative to correct = 7.875/8 × 100 = 98.4375% ≈ 98.44%.

text: Varun gave \(60\%\) of the money he had to his wife. He also gave \(20\%\) of the remaining amount to each of his three children. Three-fifth of the amount now left was spent on miscellaneous items, and the remaining amount of \(\text{₹}9,600\) was deposited in the bank. How much money did Varun have initially?
solution: Let initial = M. After wife: 0.4M. After 3 children: each gets 20% of remaining, total given = 0.6 × 0.4M = 0.24M. Remaining = 0.4M - 0.24M = 0.16M. Three-fifth spent = 0.6 × 0.16M = 0.096M, left = 0.064M = 9600 ⇒ M = 9600/0.064 = 150000.

text: Last year, 300 students appeared for an examination from school A, and \(33\frac{1}{3}\%\) of the students failed. In the same year, 480 students appeared for the examination from school B. \(60\%\) of the total students from schools A and B passed. What is the fail percentage of students from school B?
solution: A failed = 33⅓% of 300 = 100, so A passed = 200. Total students = 780. Total passed = 60% of 780 = 468. B passed = 468 - 200 = 268. B failed = 480 - 268 = 212. Fail % = 212/480 × 100 = 44.1667% = 44⅙%.

text: At a recruitment test, the candidates were tested for General Awareness (GA) and Quantitative Techniques (QT). \(54\%\) and \(45\%\) failed in GA and QT, respectively, while \(16\%\) failed in both. If 136 candidates passed in both, what was the total number of candidates?
solution: Failed in at least one = 54 + 45 - 16 = 83%. Passed in both = 17% = 136. Total = 136/0.17 = 800.

text: When the price of sugar gets raised by \(30\%\) a person increases his expenditure on sugar by \(12\%\). By what percentage (correct up to two decimal places) should he reduce his consumption of sugar so as to be able to maintain the same level of expenditure?
solution: Let initial price = p, consumption = c, expenditure = pc. New price = 1.3p, new expenditure = 1.12pc. New consumption = 1.12pc/(1.3p) = 1.12/1.3 × c = 0.86154c. Reduction = 1 - 0.86154 = 0.13846 = 13.85%.

text: By what percentage is the percentage of the number of vowels in the word CONSONANT less than the percentage of consonants in it?
solution: CONSONANT has 9 letters: vowels = O, O, A = 3, consonants = 6. Vowel % = 33.33%, consonant % = 66.67%. Vowel % less than consonant % by 33.34%. Percentage less = (33.34/66.67)×100 = 50%.

text: Chamalani, Arshad and Jagjit Singh contested an election. All the votes polled were valid. Arshad got \(35\%\) of the total votes. For every 35 votes Chamalani got 14 votes. The winner got 4950 more votes than the person who received the least number of votes. Find the total number of votes polled.
solution: Let total = T. Arshad = 0.35T. Chamalani : Jagjit ratio = 14:35 = 2:5. So Chamalani = (2/7)×0.65T = (1.3/7)T ≈ 0.1857T, Jagjit = (5/7)×0.65T = (3.25/7)T ≈ 0.4643T. Jagjit is winner, Chamalani least. Difference = 0.4643T - 0.1857T = 0.2786T = 4950 ⇒ T ≈ 17770. Not matching. Given answer key shows 33000.

text: If a number is first increased by \(15\%\) then reduced by \(15\%\) it results in 782. If the same number is first reduced by \(25\%\) then increased by \(25\%\) and again reduced by \(20\%\) then what will be the resulting number?

In a class the ratio of rural to urban students is 4 : 7. In an examination the average percentage marks of the rural and the urban students are respectively 65 and 63. What is the overall average percentage marks of the class (correct to two decimal places)?

Rajan spent \(10\%\) of his salary on rent. He spent \(20\%\) of the remaining part of the salary on transport. After which he spent \(40\%\) of the balance of the salary on further, he spends \(80\%\) of the balance on various bills. He deposits Rs.5000 in the bank and kept the remaining Rs.1480 for his own petty expenditure. Find the monthly salary (in Rs).

A sports-goods shop has tennis balls of 3 colours - red, green and white. The number of white balls is \(60\%\) more than the number of red balls and the number of green balls is \(12.5\%\) less than the number of white balls. If the total number of balls is 120, then how many green balls are there?

The present population of a village is 15280. If the number of males increases by \(25\%\) and the number of females increases by \(15\%\) then the population will become 18428. The difference between the present population of males and females in the village is:
solution: Let males = M, females = F. M+F=15280. 1.25M + 1.15F = 18428. Multiply first by 1.15: 1.15M+1.15F=17572. Subtract: 0.1M = 856 ⇒ M=8560, F=6720. Difference = 1840.

text: Price of a one gram gold coin decreased by \(10\%\) on its initial price on Monday and increased by \(20\%\) on Tuesday and again increased by \(8\%\) on Wednesday, and \(5\%\) increase on Thursday. If the final price on Thursday is Rs.5511.24, then the initial price (in Rs.) of one gram gold coin on Monday was?

In an examination of 180 marks of subjects, \(75\%\) marks are allocated for theory and the rest for project work. Veena scored \(60\%\) in theory. What percentage of marks must she score in the project to achieve an overall \(62\%\) marks in the subject?
solution: Theory max = 0.75×180=135, project max=45. Veena theory = 0.6×135=81. Total needed = 0.62×180=111.6. Project needed = 111.6-81=30.6. Percentage in project = 30.6/45×100=68%.

text: The expenditure on food is \(35\%\) of the income of a particular family. If the income is raised by \(30\%\), then how much percentage (to the nearest whole number) of food expenditure is less than the initial percentage to keep the food expenditure unchanged?
solution: Let income = 100, food = 35. New income = 130. To keep food same (35), new percentage = 35/130 × 100 = 26.92%. Less than initial by 35 - 26.92 = 8.08 percentage points. Percentage less relative to initial = 8.08/35 × 100 = 23.09% ≈ 23%.

text: The price of an article increases by \(5\%\) every year. If the difference between its price at the end of the second and the third year is Rs.52.5, then what will be its price at the end of the first year?
solution: Let initial = P. End of year 2 = P×(1.05)² = 1.1025P. End of year 3 = 1.157625P. Difference = 0.055125P = 52.5 ⇒ P = 952.38. End of year 1 = 1.05P = 1000.
